primary_error.message: the type of the other operand is a non-null type, so this comparison with 'null' will always return 'true'

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Redundant null checks on non-nullable array types cause compilation warnings.

problem_details.what: The method includes preconditions 'requires a != null && b != null' for parameters declared as 'array<int>'.
problem_details.why: In modern Dafny, 'array<int>' is a non-null type by default. Comparing non-null types with 'null' always returns true, making these checks redundant and causing compilation warnings when --allow-warnings is false.
problem_details.how: Remove the redundant 'requires a != null && b != null' precondition. The non-null guarantee is already enforced by the type system.
